content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 3, 1994

Walter W. Hansell
Cooper, White & Cooper
201 California Street, 17th Floor
San Francisco, CA 94111

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. G-94-029

Dear Mr. Hansell:

This is in response to your letter requesting information
regarding advice provided to Charles Vose, City Attorney of
Hermosa Beach. We have construed your letter to be a public
records request for information pertaining to the advice.
Enclosed please find a copy of notes taken during my conversation
with Mr. Vose.

In addition, we have enclosed some general materials
concerning the questions you have raised in your letter:

o In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198 (discussing
foreseeability).

o Regulation 18702.2 (sets forth the materiality standards
applicable to business entities).

o Bell Advice Letter, No. I-93-337 (discussing the
Commission's role in providing advice; the Commission does
not act as the finder of fact).

o Regulation 18329 (discussing the distinctions between
Commission formal and informal advice) See also, Section
83114.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. G-94-029
Page 2


However, as you are aware, the Political Reform Act requires
the Commission to provide advice only to persons whose duties
under the Act are in question, or to that person's authorized
representative. (Section 83114(b).) The Commission does not
provide advice to third parties either formally or informally
concerning another person's duties under the Act. (Regulation
18329(b)(8).) Thus, we have not evaluated the facts and materials
you submitted with your letter.

If you believe a violation of the Act has occurred you should
contact the Commission's Enforcement Division at (916) 322-6441.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide advice to a third party regarding another person's duties under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide advice to third parties regarding another person's duties under the Political Reform Act; advice is only provided to the person whose duties are in question or their authorized representative.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a response to a public records request rather than a traditional advice letter seeking guidance. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The substantive legal principle is embedded in the body text explaining FPPC's limitations under Section 83114(b) and Regulation 18329(b)(8).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide advice to a third party regarding another person's duties under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide advice to third parties regarding another person's duties under the Political Reform Act; advice is only provided to the person whose duties are in question or their authorized representative.

In addition, we have enclosed some general materials concerning the questions you have raised in your letter: o In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198 (discussing foreseeability). o Regulation 18702.2 (sets forth the materiality standards applicable to business entities). o Bell Advice Letter, No. I-93-337 (discussing the Commission's role in providing advice; the Commission does not act as the finder of fact). o Regulation 18329 (discussing the distinctions between Commission formal and informal advice) See also, Section 83114. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Thus, we have not evaluated the facts and materials you submitted with your letter. If you believe a violation of the Act has occurred you should contact the Commission's Enforcement Division at (916) 322-6441. Sincerely, John W. Wallace Counsel, Legal Division
embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a public records request from an attorney requesting information about advice previously provided to a City Attorney, clarifying that it cannot evaluate facts or provide guidance to third parties regarding another person's Political Reform Act duties.
